title: Avoidable same-direction strand crossings inside a bend group
summary: The image shows a same-direction group of fiber strands bending through the routing zone with avoidable lane crossings, broken nesting, and visible color-order disruption. The strands should stay in a clean nested bundle and preserve the standard fiber strand color-code order unless a higher-priority routing constraint forces a controlled local exception.
visually_observable_bad_behavior: Multiple same-direction strands make staggered 90-degree bends that cause the routes to pass through or over nearby strand lanes.
The strand group does not remain nested as an organized adjacent lane band through the bend area.
The visible strand order changes through the bend instead of preserving the standard Blue, Orange, Green, Brown sequence for this visible group.
The bend pattern creates unnecessary crossing and visual lane swapping even though the strands are moving in the same general direction.
The group is harder to trace because related strands do not keep a simple outside-to-inside nested bend relationship.

broken_requirements: Related fiber strands should stay visually grouped as they move through the splice detail diagram.
Nested strands should use adjacent routing lanes and move through the routing zone as an organized group.
A strand should not cross through an unrelated or nearby group unnecessarily.
likely_broken_rules.why_this_image_fails: The visible strands bend in a way that breaks the nested lane relationship and creates avoidable lane swapping/crossing inside the group.

broken_requirements: Fiber strands should display in standard fiber optic color-code order inside each buffer tube, fan out, strand group, and nested route group.
Nested routing should preserve strand order when assigning nearby lanes inside a buffer tube group.
Strand order should be adjusted only when required to avoid a higher-priority routing failure.
likely_broken_rules.why_this_image_fails: The visible bend area disrupts the expected Blue, Orange, Green, Brown lane order instead of maintaining the ordered nested bundle.

broken_requirements: Individual fiber strand routes must maintain the 24px grid pitch in all directions.
Spacing applies around bends and lane transitions.
Strands should remain visually separated and traceable without avoidable congestion.
likely_broken_rules.why_this_image_fails: The staggered bend pattern collapses the readable spacing relationship at the bend and makes the routes appear to cut through neighboring strand lanes.

likely_broken_rules.rule_name: Fiber Strand Overlap, Crossing, and Collision
broken_requirements: Overlap, collision, and illegal shared lanes must be prevented or flagged.
A crossing should only be used as a controlled exception when required to reach the splice target.
Avoidable crossings should be cleaned up before the layout is accepted.
likely_broken_rules.why_this_image_fails: The crossing appears avoidable because the strands travel in the same general direction and should be routable as a nested ordered bend.

broken_requirements: The routing engine should use available space instead of creating avoidable congestion.
Routes must remain traceable through the routing zone.
Routes should spread strand groups apart instead of stacking confusing transitions into a tight area.
likely_broken_rules.why_this_image_fails: The bend area uses the routing zone in a congested way even though the group should be traceable as a clean nested same-direction bend.

allowed_exception_context.exception_summary: A group may need this type of disrupted bend only when the group starts and ends on the same side of the diagram and must loop back.
allowed_exception_context.why_exception_does_not_appear_to_apply: The visible crop does not show a same-side loopback requirement, and the user identified the crossings as unnecessary. In the normal same-direction bend case, one side of the group should preserve normal nesting and color-code order rather than crossing through itself.

notes: Use this as a bad reference for avoidable crossings inside a same-direction bend group.
This example contrasts with good nested-bend examples where strand lanes preserve color order and bend as a clean bundle.

The screenshot is cropped; the full CSV-derived cable, buffer tube, fan-out, and splice-dot context is not visible, so the same-side loopback exception is recorded as a possible but unsupported exception rather than as the likely cause.
